title: Development Considerations for Performance and Scalability
breadcrumbs: Development Considerations for Performance and Scalability
text: Development Considerations for Performance and Scalability During development, the focus of performance optimization measures is primarily on component design and ProcScript coding. For example, aim to do the following: Implement asynchronous or stateless requests whenever possible Retrieve appropriate amounts of data. For example, use stepped hitlists, or create a service to return only the entity field required rather than all fields Reuse retrieved data rather than requesting it each time. Use correct data types to reduce conversions Implement data processing at the appropriate level (entity or occurrence) Use include ProcScript operations in preference to global ProcScript operations. This can improve encapsulation, which in turn assists with flexibility in deployment Related Links Component Memory Management At runtime, component instances require memory to handle the component definition and component data. This memory is not necessarily freed when the component instance is deleted. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
